title: quercus chrysolepis subsp. vacciniifolia
intro: Quercus chrysolepis subsp. vacciniifolia, also known as the Oak-leaf Mountain Mahogany, is a subspecies of evergreen shrub or small tree in the family Fagaceae. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Quercus chrysolepis subsp. vacciniifolia include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and support immune function, among others. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
